_meta.source_listings: Mario Francella (Royal LePage) forwarded MLS feed 2026-06-13
_meta.notes: Building specs verified across condos.ca, strata.ca, condonow.com, talkcondo.com. Listing-specific data pulled from realtor.ca + condos.ca. Walk/transit scores from talkcondo + walkscore data. Honest takes calibrated to Fadi: 27yo ER attending @ Credit Valley, drives daily (12pm-6am shifts), 100km/wk runner, recently single, wants Toronto over Mississauga.

1169_queen_st_w_525n.building_name: Bohemian Embassy Condos (1169 Queen W tower)
building_pros: Pemberton Group is a reputable developer (vs. Urbancorp at Edge)
1169 = the lower mid-rise (9 storeys) = quieter than the 19-storey 1171 tower
Soft loft layout with 9ft ceilings, floor-to-ceiling windows
Walk Score 95, Transit Score 100 - on the 501 Queen streetcar at the door
Heart of West Queen West / Little Portugal - dining, nightlife, dating density very high
Trinity Bellwoods 10min walk - excellent for runs
Lakeshore / MGT trail 15min south for long runs
building_cons: 1169 tower is on the QUEEN ST side - streetcar + traffic noise (north exposure faces Queen)
No subway within easy walk - nearest is Dufferin Stn (Line 2) ~17min walk or 5min streetcar
Lots of investor/rental units = some wear and tenant turnover
$2,400 at top end of 1BR comparable rents but well within budget
1169_queen_st_w_525n.honest_take_for_fadi: Solid pick. Bohemian Embassy is a real building by a real developer (Pemberton, not Urbancorp), and you're paying $2,400 to live on Queen W with streetcar at the door. The 1169 tower is the quieter mid-rise (9 stories) vs. the 19-story sister tower at 1171. North exposure means you face Queen Street though - expect streetcar dings and bar-close noise at 2am, which is moot for your 12pm-6am shift schedule but bad if you're trying to sleep mornings. WORTH TOURING. Ask about: noise from Queen, locker inclusion (listing didn't confirm), balcony size, and whether 525N is the front or rear of the tower.

36_lisgar_st_lph03e.building_name: Edge on Triangle Park - West Tower
36_lisgar_st_lph03e.exposure: E (the 'E' in LPH03E = East exposure; W variant LPH03W was listed at $2,600 prior)
building_pros: Across from the Drake Hotel - peak Queen W culture
Indoor pool is rare at this price point
Lower penthouse floor = high views, less street noise
Split 2BR/2BA layout - if you want roommate or office, can host Nomi visits + have a study
$2,500 for 2BR in this location is genuinely well below market - the 2BR/2BA at LPH03W listed for $2,600 same building
Triangle Park (parkette) directly outside
building_cons: URBANCORP - developer went bankrupt 2016; building has reputation issues. TikTok '#blacklisted' references exist for 36 Lisgar specifically
500-599 sqft for a 2BR is TIGHT - that's why the price is low. Bedrooms will be small, no functional dining space
660 units, lots of tenants, high turnover, more wear and elevator waits
Past crime reports per Google searches (typical Queen/Dufferin)
Queen + Dufferin is loud + busy until 2am
Same subway-distance gap as 1169 Queen (~17min walk to Dufferin Stn)
36_lisgar_st_lph03e.honest_take_for_fadi: The $2,500 is NOT a fluke - it's a 500-599sqft 2BR (basically a 1BR with a partition wall) in an Urbancorp building with reputation baggage. The 'lower penthouse' designation is mostly marketing for floor 20/21 of 21. East exposure means morning sun = good for your 12pm-6am inverted schedule (you sleep through morning glare). The split 2-bath is genuinely useful if Nomi visits. TOUR ONLY IF you've personally walked Edge on Triangle Park before - the building's tenant-heavy social atmosphere is hit-or-miss. Otherwise, the per-sqft value is real but the building isn't. Ask: which floor is LPH03 actually on, what the pool/gym condition is, current maintenance fee disputes.

20_gladstone_ave_201.exposure: Unit 201 = 2nd floor, likely east/Gladstone side (low floor)
20_gladstone_ave_201.parking_detail: Not confirmed in listing - ask Mario
20_gladstone_ave_201.mario_note: may not be available - Mario was confirming
building_pros: Streetcar Developments - boutique builder with good Queen W reputation (Carnaby, Edition, Garrison etc.)
Only 128 units = quieter, better community feel vs. Edge (660) or Bohemian (364)
Authentic soft loft: 9ft EXPOSED CONCRETE ceilings, large windows, hardwood
Metro grocery in the building = grocery run = elevator ride
$2,600 with 1+1 (den functional as office/study/exam-prep room for Sept 2026 CCFP-EM)
Recently built (2016) = newer systems vs. Edge (2014)
building_cons: Unit 201 = 2nd floor = low view, possibly some street/sidewalk noise
Exposed concrete ceilings look great but are NOISY - sound bounces (matters for 12pm-6am sleep)
$2,600 same price as 270 Dufferin (newer build with pool/gym) - Twenty Lofts amenity set is thinner (no gym, no pool)
Parking/locker not confirmed - if neither included, value drops significantly
Mario flagged it may already be gone - verify availability before scheduling tour
No subway nearby - same 17min walk to Dufferin Stn
20_gladstone_ave_201.honest_take_for_fadi: Best-builder-on-the-list pick (Streetcar > Pemberton > Lifetime > Urbancorp in West End reputation). The den is real value for exam prep. But: 2nd floor unit + exposed concrete ceilings = noise concerns for someone post-night-shift. Confirm with Mario that it's still available BEFORE you build expectations - he flagged it. If available AND parking is included, this is your sleeper pick. Ask: parking, locker, balcony presence, what's directly below (Metro loading dock?), what time deliveries start.

building_pros: NEWEST build of the 5 (2023) - modern finishes, quartz counters, built-in appliances, full 4-pc ensuite in primary
South exposure = best light + lake glimpse possible from floor 8
Lifetime Developments = solid mid-tier (XO, Wonder Condos, etc.)
2 FULL bathrooms in a 1+1 is rare - massive value for visitor flexibility
Closest drive to CVH of the 5 (36.7min Mon 7am) and shortest km (29.93km)
Walk Score 100/100 - on the King streetcar 24hr line
Building amenity set is the deepest (gym + yoga + library + business centre + on-site restaurant)
Building rentals average -4.42% off ask = landlord likely negotiable further from $2,600
building_cons: NO PETS - hard cutoff if you ever wanted a dog/cat
NO LOCKER - storage limited to unit only
Parkdale stigma persists - though South Parkdale (south of King) is rapidly gentrifying, walking north of Queen at night feels different
38 DOM + price drop = something is keeping renters away. Possible causes: pet ban, no locker, south-facing means it's HOT in summer with full sun + low blinds
500-599 sqft for 1+1 with 2 baths means the den is small and bedrooms tight
Dufferin/King intersection has 24hr Dufferin bus noise + King streetcar
270_dufferin_st_807.nearest_subway_name: Dufferin Stn (Line 2) - or Exhibition GO 12min walk
270_dufferin_st_807.honest_take_for_fadi: STRONGEST NEGOTIATION POSITION of the 5. 38 days on market + already dropped means landlord is bleeding. Newest building (2023), best amenity stack, closest drive to CVH, 2 full bathrooms in a 1+1 - this is genuinely the best feature set if you can live with NO PETS and NO LOCKER. Counter-offer $2,400-2,500 confidently; they're more likely to take a tenant TODAY than wait for $2,600. Real risk: south exposure on floor 8 = potential heat issue, ask about A/C zoning. TOUR THIS. Ask: actual sqft on floor plan, why it's been sitting (politely), whether locker can be added from rental pool, summer cooling performance.

20_gladstone_ave_405.exposure: Unit 405 = 4th floor of 7, likely Gladstone/east side based on stack
20_gladstone_ave_405.parking_detail: Not confirmed - Twenty Lofts unit 317 had parking+locker; verify 405
building_pros: Same building strengths as Unit 201 (Streetcar, small 128-unit boutique, Metro downstairs, 9ft exposed concrete ceilings, hardwood)
TRUE 2BR (not 1+den) at $2,700 - $100 more than the 1+den at 201, gets you a real second bedroom
Mid-floor (4 of 7) = better light than 201, less street noise
Only 1 bathroom is the trade-off vs. 36 Lisgar LPH03E - matters less if living solo
Comparable Twenty 2BR units (Unit 311, Unit 416, Unit 617) listed $2,800-2,850 - $2,700 is the lowest currently
DOM=4 means fresh on market, no negotiation leverage yet but also no red flag
building_cons: $2,700 for 1 bath is awkward if you want guests/Nomi long-stay
Exposed concrete = same noise echo concern as 201 (matters for shift sleep)
Parking/locker unconfirmed - critical to verify for the price
Twenty Lofts has thinner amenity set than XO or Edge (no gym, no pool)
Same subway gap (~17min to Dufferin Stn)
20_gladstone_ave_405.honest_take_for_fadi: True 2BR in a small boutique loft building by the best developer on the list, at $2,700 (lowest of any current Twenty 2BR). Mid-floor placement, real loft aesthetic with exposed concrete and hardwood. If you confirm parking + locker are included, this is arguably the strongest pick on the list IF you value the second bedroom (exam study + Nomi visits) over a second bathroom. TOUR. Ask: parking + locker, true sqft, exposure (Gladstone-side vs. courtyard-side), what's above/below the unit (4 of 7 means real neighbours both directions), whether the second bedroom has a window.
